mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which signals immediate economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of greater than 1% month-over-month are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered periods over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate profit margins, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following an INDPRO drop exceeding the threshold.
